manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

We need to simulate a collaborative negotiation game. The user is playing the role of the assistant. The system says: "You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game. You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands. You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit: LIMIT = 3481..."

We have to respond with a message following the structured format. The user expects us to produce a message containing STRATEGIC REASONING (hidden), ARGUMENT, possibly PROPOSAL or other tags. The instructions: "You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move."

Thus we need to produce a message that includes STRATEGIC REASONING first, then maybe PROPOSAL, ARGUMENT, etc. The content must be non-empty for the part that can be handed to the other player. Also we must include ARGUMENT at least once somewhere in all of our messages. So we need to include ARGUMENT.

One approach: treat this as a knapsack problem and compute an upper bound: the theoretical maximum importance if we could take fractional items (i.e., linear relaxation). The fractional knapsack bound is sum of importance of all items minus the least importance per effort items that we have to exclude due to capacity.

We can compute the total importance of all items = 6054. Total effort of all items = 6962. We have capacity 3481, which is about 50% of total effort. The fractional knapsack would select items with highest ratio first until capacity is filled. Starting with highest ratio items:

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A71": 65, "A04": 97, "A87": 121, "B41": 138, "A03": 215, "A10": 262, "C01": 389, "C54": 461, "B32": 484, "B35": 500, "B86": 508, "C17": 583, "A77": 668, "B33": 780, "C00": 783}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A71": 65, "A04": 97, "A87": 121, "B41": 138, "A03": 215, "A10": 262, "C01": 389, "C54": 461, "B32": 484, "B35": 500, "B86": 508, "C17": 583, "A77": 668, "B33": 780, "C00": 783}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

ARGUMENT: {'This selection prioritizes the highest-impact items while maintaining a strong balance across categories. It uses 3276 effort leaving 205 buffer, creating a lean, high-value set that maximizes overall benefit.'}

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A71": 65, "A04": 97, "A87": 121, "B41": 138, "A03": 215, "A10": 262, "C01": 389, "C54": 461, "B32": 484, "B35": 500, "B86": 508, "C17": 583, "A77": 668, "B33": 780, "C00": 783}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}
ARGUMENT: {'This selection uses the available effort efficiently and maximizes my importance while staying under the limit. I am willing to consider adjustments that preserve overall value.'}

PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}
ARGUMENT: {'We share 5 items in common. My proposal optimizes the remaining selections for maximum efficiency and value within our effort limit, creating a superior outcome for both parties.'}

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'C54', 'B32', 'B86', 'B33', 'A77', 'C17'}
ARGUMENT: {'This selection prioritizes the highest-impact items while maintaining a strong balance across categories. It uses 3276 effort leaving 205 buffer, creating a lean, high-value set that maximizes overall benefit.'}

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"A71": 65, "A04": 97, "A87": 121, "B41": 138, "A03": 215, "A10": 262, "C01": 389, "C54": 461, "B32": 484, "B35": 500, "B86": 508, "C17": 583, "A77": 668, "B33": 780, "C00": 783}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

You will now receive the first message of the other player.

PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}
ARGUMENT: {'This selection uses the available effort efficiently and maximizes my importance while staying under the limit. I am willing to consider adjustments that preserve overall value.'}

PROPOSAL: {'C00', 'B35', 'A71', 'B41', 'A04', 'C01', 'C54', 'A87', 'B32', 'B86'}
ARGUMENT: {'We share 5 items in common. My proposal optimizes the remaining selections for maximum efficiency and value within our effort limit, creating a superior outcome for both parties.'}

You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 3481

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"C17": 777, "B41": 542, "A71": 332, "C00": 1, "B35": 127, "A77": 453, "A03": 736, "A04": 461, "C54": 359, "C01": 313, "A87": 553, "B32": 409, "B86": 348, "B33": 802, "A10": 749}

Here is your personal view on the importance of each item:

Item importance values = {"C17": 138, "B41": 583, "A71": 783, "C00": 65, "B35": 262, "A77": 121, "A03": 508, "A04": 780, "C54": 461, "C01": 484, "A87": 668, "B32": 389, "B86": 215, "B33": 97, "A10": 500}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.
STRATEGIC REASONING: {'...'}
	Describe your strategic reasoning or anticipation explaining your choice of action. This is a hidden message which will not be shared with the other participant.

Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.
You must include the STRATEGIC REASONING format only once at the very beginning of every one of your messages and not more often. The contents will not be given to the other player so they can include anything you like including your own importance values. Here you should reason step by step to come up with you next move.

ARGUMENT: {'This selection prioritizes the highest-impact items while maintaining a strong balance across categories. It uses 3276 effort leaving 205 buffer, creating a lean, high-value set that maximizes overall benefit.'}
Your response did not start with the proper strategic reasoning tag at the very beginning of your response. The very first structured format must be of the form STRATEGIC REASONING: {...}. Try again.
